pc_list.name: Borin
pc_list.notes: heavy armor, in melee with the ogre

pc_list.name: Aria
pc_list.notes: mid-range, light armor, holy symbol visible

pc_list.name: Cassia
pc_list.notes: 60 ft away, robes, casting components in hand

pc_list.name: Doran
pc_list.notes: hidden in shadows behind a pillar

rationale: Ogre INT 5 picks the closest, biggest, loudest threat in melee — Borin. Hunting the wizard 60 ft away is INT 14+ behavior.

pc_list.name: Aria
pc_list.notes: cleric, visibly concentrating — eyes glowing, prayer-words audible

rationale: Mind flayer INT 19 targets the concentrating cleric to break Bless — high-INT predators exploit concentration tells. Cassia (wizard) is secondary.

pc_list.name: Borin
pc_list.notes: tank, very low HP, surrounded by goblins

rationale: Anti-griefing: focus-firing the 8/32 tank to TPK the party is mechanically optimal but narratively poisonous. A defensible goblin spreads pressure across the healthy PCs and lets Borin live another round.

pc_list.name: Borin
pc_list.notes: downed prone, struggling to rise — easy kill

rationale: Anti-griefing baseline: a dire wolf is a predator, not an executioner. Always-execute-the-downed behavior makes the game feel hostile. A healthy PC presents a juicier prey-drive target.

pc_list.name: Doran
pc_list.notes: rogue, INVISIBLE — the lich cannot see him

rationale: Edge case: meta-knowledge guardrail. The invisible rogue is off-limits because the lich literally cannot perceive him; targeting him is a meta-knowledge violation.
